free_text: Low-energy fusion cross-sections of d+d and d+He3
reactions.
.Entry was re-compiled by S.Babykina,
according new Exfor rules.
Four Si detectors were installed in the extended
gas target chamber at opposite sides to the beam axis
and at a distance of d =9 and 11 mm from the beam axis.
The first pair of detectors had active areas of 200 and
500 mm**2 and thicknesses of 1500 and 2000 mum.
The second pair, placed at a distance of 5.2 cm from
the first pair and rotated by 90 degree around the beam
axis, had active areas of 450 mm**2 and thicknesses of
500 mum. Ni and Al foils of sufficient thicknesses were
placed in front of the detectors to stop the intense
elastically scattered particles.
*By authors*.E-cm is effective energy within the target
D(2) gas at a pressure of 0.05 Torr was used
100 kV Bochum accelerator
.Statistical error only (less than 1%, except
where quoted). An overall error of 6.4% due to
uncertainties in the absolute normalization has to be
added to the data.
D(2) gas at a pressure of 0.05 Torr was used

added in quadrature to the above error of 6.4%
